title: Getting Started
description: Set up your environment and learn what you need to get started

blocks.content:
To create web pages with HTML and CSS, you only need two things:

1. **A Text Editor** - Software where you write code
2. **A Web Browser** - Software to view your web pages

That's it! You don't need anything fancy or expensive.

- **Visual Studio Code** (Free, powerful, beginner-friendly) - https://code.visualstudio.com
- **Notepad++** (Windows only, lightweight)
- **Sublime Text** (Cross-platform, simple)
- Even your computer's built-in notepad works!

We recommend VS Code for beginners - it has great features that help you learn.

Any modern browser works great:
- **Google Chrome**
- **Firefox**
- **Safari** (Mac)
- **Edge** (Windows)

Chrome is recommended for beginners because it has excellent developer tools.

Each lesson includes:
- **Explanations** of concepts
- **Code examples** you can study
- **Try it yourself** sections where you write code
- **Tips and tricks** to help you learn faster

We start with the basics and gradually build up to creating a complete multi-page website.
**Pro Tip**: Code along with every example. Don't just read - actually type the code yourself. This helps your brain learn faster!

By the end of this course, you'll be able to:

✅ Write basic HTML to create web page structure  
✅ Use CSS to style your pages with colors, fonts, and layouts  
✅ Create a multi-page website  
✅ Understand how the web works  
✅ Build projects from scratch

Let's get started! Head over to the HTML Basics page to begin your journey.
